title: Devi Mahatmya Stotra
intro: A sacred hymn praising the divine energy and power of the Divine Mother, recited for protection and blessings.
description:
The Devi Mahatmya Stotra is a powerful hymn that celebrates the victories of the Divine Mother over evil forces. It contains verses praising her various forms and attributes.

|| Stotra ||
Ya Devi Sarva Bhuteshu, Shakti Rupena Samsthita.
Namastasyai Namastasyai, Namastasyai Namo Namah.

This stotra is traditionally recited during Navratri festival and throughout the year for divine protection and spiritual strength.
meaning: This stotra honors the Divine Mother as the eternal source of power and energy that pervades all existence. It seeks her blessings for protection from negative forces.

faq.q1: When is this stotra most powerful?
faq.a1: During Navratri festival (nine days celebrating the Divine Mother), but can be recited anytime.
faq.q2: How many times should it be recited?
faq.a2: Traditionally recited 108 times, but even daily recitation brings benefits.
faq.a3: Divine protection, spiritual growth, and overcoming obstacles in life.
